markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define how a no-context model resumes from WP/MT state, receipts, git status, EventLedger, and UserManual without chat history. This MT belongs to ParallelSwarmStateRecovery. Make WP-009 safe for local/cloud swarm work, backend navigation, and repeated session recovery.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Local and cloud swarm lanes must be observable, attributable, bounded, recoverable, and safe for parallel agents.
Every MT must leave enough receipts, state markers, and next-step context for session-limit recovery by a no-context model.
scope.expected_behavior: SessionCompactionRecoveryFlow leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: SessionCompactionRecoveryFlow is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

commits.slice: Parallel swarm state recovery hardening for MT-209..216

evidence.summary: MT-209..216 implement lane/capability model, claim/lease receipts, mailbox/handoff/checkpoint/recovery atomicity, recovery/reclaim behavior, attribution scrub, and EventLedger-backed state recovery surfaces.

commands.result: PASS; 16/16 parallel swarm state recovery tests passed
PASS; test target registered without duplicate names

evidence.summary: Reviewer passed MT-209..216 after capability, lease, mailbox, receipt, handoff/checkpoint/recovery, and claim/release atomicity hardening.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-214 (SessionCompactionRecoveryFlow) requires a restartable PostgreSQL/EventLedger-backed checkpoint+recovery flow that lets a no-context model resume from durable state. The code implements real checkpoint recording and hash-verified recovery against managed PostgreSQL with EventLedger-linked receipts, and tests exercise the real native path including corruption/missing/authority-failure negatives. It satisfies the MT and aligns with the current Master Spec.
findings: state_recovery.rs:2851 record_checkpoint persists resume_pointer, touched_files, tests, hbr_rows, next_step_context, payload_sha256, compaction_reason, git_head into knowledge_agent_state_recovery_checkpoints inside a single tx that also appends a KnowledgeCrdtCheckpointRecorded EventLedger event (line 2862) - exactly the no-context restartable state MT-214 demands.

state_recovery.rs:2932 recover_from_checkpoint recomputes the payload SHA-256 (2947) and returns StateRecoveryError::PayloadHashMismatch (2949) before emitting any recovery receipt, then writes knowledge_agent_recovery_receipts with prior/new session lineage and an EventLedger event in one tx - matches Master Spec 02-system-architecture.md:6156/6166/6177 (immutable checkpoints, explicit checkpoint-restore recovery, resume from bounded state without transcript reconstruction).

migrations/0311_parallel_swarm_state_recovery.sql:62-110 creates real PostgreSQL tables with FK REFERENCES kernel_event_ledger(event_id) and a payload_sha256 CHECK regex; registers MT-214 family parallel_swarm_recovery_receipts in knowledge_schema_registry (line 155). No SQLite, no sidecar.

tests/parallel_swarm_state_recovery_tests.rs:174 recovery_store() builds on knowledge_pg() (knowledge_pg_support.rs:108) which connects to a real PostgreSQL base URL, creates an isolated per-test schema, and runs the full migration chain (run_migrations, line 156); tests SKIP (not fake) when no PG is configured.

Negative proof is real and resource-touching: mt223_corrupt_checkpoint_payload_hash_does_not_emit_recovery_receipt (4303) records a checkpoint, corrupts payload_jsonb via raw SQL UPDATE, asserts recovery returns PayloadHashMismatch, and asserts 0 recovery receipts AND 0 ledger recovery events; mt223_missing_checkpoint_recovery (4264) and recovery_receipt_authority_failure (5220) cover further negatives.

mailbox_navigation_checkpoint_and_recovery_are_restartable_from_postgres (3815) proves a cloud->local lane resume round-trip: resume_pointer and touched_files survive, receipt records prior/new session, no provider secret leaks, exactly 1 receipt persisted in PostgreSQL.
No deferred-live escapes: grep of state_recovery.rs found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/*Unavailable/'deferred to follow-on'/'pending MT-' on any path.
Sub-rule 3: lifecycle.claimed_by=null and completed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER-20260612 (not identical self-cert at contract level); the proof is independently real regardless.

Aside (non-blocking, expected): packet cites v02.192 anchors and the spec's legacy durable-execution narrative still mentions SQLite (02-system-architecture.md:8447), but the MT/packet constraints correctly mandate PostgreSQL and the impl complies - stale spec pointer is paperwork, not a code defect.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
